アーティストは生きる死人である

世の人からすれば、アーティストの犠牲は徹底的に無視しなければ自分が生き残れないものである

施政者は「本当に犠牲となったものだけが被害を訴えなさい」というだろう。しかし、その時には本当に被害を受けた者は沈黙している

言うほどにウ～ンラッダイト笑とか言われながらアーティスト側へのハラスメントの犬笛が高まるのだから、
やらんですむなら本当にやりたくないが、やらないわけにもいかないのだよな…

A Look at Jeff Beck’s Career and Studio Portraits During His Transformative 1985 Period

🤖 IA: It's not clickbait ✅
👥 Users: It's not clickbait ✅

#jeffbeck #guitarist #rockmusic 

View full AI summary:

external.description: The article presents a collection of 30 studio portraits of legendary English guitarist Jeff Beck taken in 1985 while also examining an important stage in his musical career. Beck, who first gained recognition with The Yardbirds before leading the Jeff Beck Group and Beck, Bogert & Appice, had already established himself as one of rock music’s most innovative guitarists. By the mid-1980s, he was exploring a new artistic direction that blended his signature guitar style with the polished production techniques and synthesizer-driven sounds that characterized the decade. In 1985, Beck released the album 'Flash' after a five-year break from solo recordings. Produced largely by Nile Rodgers and Arthur Baker, the album pursued a more commercial approach while still showcasing Beck’s exceptional guitar skills. One of its highlights was a reunion with Rod Stewart on the song 'People Get Ready,' which became a major success and received extensive exposure on MTV. The instrumental track 'Escape' earned Beck his first Grammy Award for Best Rock Instrumental Performance in 1986. During this period, Beck was also highly sought after as a session musician. He contributed guitar work to Mick Jagger’s debut solo album 'She’s the Boss,' Tina Turner’s 'Private Dancer,' and The Honeydrippers project alongside Robert Plant, Jimmy Page, and Nile Rodgers. The article notes that Beck was refining the fingerstyle techniques and expressive use of the Stratocaster tremolo arm that later became hallmarks of his playing. It concludes by highlighting his return to a more experimental instrumental approach with 'Jeff Beck’s Guitar Shop,' which won another Grammy and reaffirmed his reputation as a groundbreaking guitarist.
external.title: A Look at Jeff Beck’s Career and Studio Portraits During His Transformative 1985 Period

Political commentator Leigh McGowan and conservative radio host Jason Rantz engaged in a heated debate during a CNN segment moderated by Abby Phillip. The discussion focused on recent Democratic primary results in New York, including the political influence of Zohran Mamdani and congressional candidate Darializa Avila Chevalier. Rantz argued that the rise of democratic socialism and what he described as antisemitic views among some left-wing candidates could become a political liability for Democrats and help Republicans in upcoming midterm elections. McGowan rejected that characterization, arguing that Rantz was presenting fringe positions as representative of the broader Democratic Party.

McGowan emphasized that many Americans are struggling economically and are looking for elected officials who prioritize ordinary voters rather than wealthy donors, lobbyists, and billionaires. She pointed to Mamdani's political messaging as an example of a candidate who has connected with voters by focusing on concrete issues and promises. Throughout the exchange, Rantz repeatedly interrupted McGowan while challenging her views and questioning whether she could identify candidates who did not hold the positions he criticized.

The interruptions became a central feature of the segment. McGowan objected several times, stating that she was unable to finish her arguments because Rantz kept talking over her. After multiple interruptions, she raised her voice and exclaimed that she was speaking and wanted the opportunity to complete her point. Moderator Abby Phillip intervened and asked Rantz to allow McGowan to finish. McGowan then concluded that Mamdani had demonstrated an ability to communicate a clear agenda and convince voters that he could deliver results, something she argued many Democrats have struggled to do.

🤖 IA: It's clickbait ⚠️
👥 Users: It's clickbait ⚠️

#trump #iranwar #senatevote 

View full AI summary:

external.description: President Donald Trump expressed strong criticism on Truth Social against four Republican senators who joined Democrats in a Senate vote to limit his authority regarding the ongoing conflict with Iran. The senators in question are Rand Paul of Kentucky, Lisa Murkowski of Alaska, Susan Collins of Maine, and Bill Cassidy of Louisiana. In a 50-48 vote, the Senate passed a resolution urging Trump to end U.S. military involvement in Iran or seek explicit congressional approval to continue operations. This measure, described by The New York Times as a significant bipartisan rebuke, lacks the force of law but highlights growing congressional unease with the conflict. Trump claimed that he had Iran 'on the ropes' and ready to concede, accusing the senators of making his job harder and providing 'aid and comfort' to the enemy by signaling division in the U.S. government. The vote reflects ongoing tensions over war powers and the president's unilateral actions in foreign policy. The resolution passed the House earlier and represents one of the more notable congressional challenges to Trump's handling of the Iran situation. Despite the non-binding nature of the vote, it underscores divisions within the Republican Party, with moderate senators breaking ranks. Trump has maintained that his approach is yielding results and vowed to proceed regardless. This episode occurs amid broader debates about U.S. military engagements and the balance of power between the executive and legislative branches. The senators who supported the resolution have previously shown willingness to cross party lines on certain issues, prioritizing constitutional oversight on military matters.
external.title: Trump Criticizes Four Republican Senators for Supporting Bipartisan Iran War Powers Resolution

images.alt:
看这个推文，检查我们的codex 有没有这个问题？并尝试修复

https://x.com/Hicker_Moledao/status/2069111296910671960




5:38






已创建 3 个文件编辑了 2 个文件正在编辑 1 个文件执行了 56 条命令已使用 Playwright个集成已创建 3 个文件编辑了 2 个文件正在编辑 1 个文件执行了 56 条命令已使用 Playwright个集成


已处理 11m 23s







本地 Codex 确实中了推文里说的这个问题——~/.codex/logs_2.sqlite 现在 287 MB（约 274 MB），而且 wal 文件还在增长。
根因定位
问题出在 codex-rs/state/src/log_db.rs 的 default_filter()：它把 SQLite 日志层的默认级别硬编码成了 LevelFilter::TRACE。控制台的 tracing-subscriber 层会读 RUST_LOG，但 SQLite 日志层用自己的过滤器，所以无论 RUST_LOG 设成什么，TRACE 事件都会被持续写入 logs_2.sqlite。
修复内容  
把 SQLite 日志默认级别从 TRACE 降到 DEBUG，直接消除最高频的 trace 写入。  
新增环境变量 CODEX_LOG_LEVEL，需要排查时可以显式恢复：export CODEX_LOG_LEVEL=trace。  
保留现有的批量插入、队列丢弃和 opentelemetry_sdk 低级别日志过滤逻辑不变。  
在 codex-rs/state/src/log_db_filter_tests.rs 增加了两个测试：确认 DEBUG 默认会丢弃 TRACE，以及显式 TRACE 时仍会保留。
验证  
cargo test -p codex-state --lib log_db
# 9 passed
完整 cargo check -p codex-app-server -p codex-tui 因为 v8 crate 在 Windows 上需要创建符号链接的特权而失败，与本次改动无关；codex-state 本身编译和测试都通过。
文档
已按项目文档规范更新：  
[`docs/plan/plan_fix-codex-trace-log-write.md`](/D:/Backup/Documents/codex-fix/codex/docs/plan/plan_fix-codex-trace-log-write.md)

你看看github 或任何平台的讨论先？我修复前先用opencode 吧... 但我才刚计划迁移到codex 的哈哈，看来codex 不欢迎我（笑




5:57






已运行 11 条命令


已处理 1m 34s







我搜了一下，结论可能让你心情好一点：OpenAI 不是没发现，而是已经在 6 月 22 日合并了修复。
GitHub 上的讨论
核心 Issue：openai/codex#28224 — Codex SQLite feedback logs can write ~640 TB/year and rapidly consume SSD endurance
这个 Issue 是 6 月 14 日提的，作者给出了非常详细的数据：
21 天 uptime 写了约 37 TB
推算约 640 TB/年
TRACE 占保留日志字节的 70.7%
主要噪声来源：codex_api::endpoint::responses_websocket TRACE（527.4 MiB）
codex_otel.log_only / codex_otel.trace_safe INFO 镜像事件
target=log 的 TRACE（inotify、tokio-tungstenite WebSocket 内部事件等）

然后 6 月 22 日合并了两个 PR 把它关了：
PR #29432 — Stop logging every Responses WebSocket event
PR #29457 — Filter noisy targets from persistent logs
PR #29457 的改动其实和我找到的地方完全一样：codex-rs/state/src/log_db.rs 里的 default_filter()。它把 target=log、codex_otel.log_only、codex_otel.trace_safe 从 SQLite 持久化中排除。Issue 作者反馈这两个 PR 能减少约 85% 的日志。
那你电脑上为什么还是 287 MB？
因为你装的稳定版是 @openai/codex@0.141.0，而这两个修复还没进 stable。npm 上最新已经是 0.142.0-alpha.12（以及对应的平台包），alpha 通道里已经包含这次修复。
所以你的情况不是“OpenAI 不欢迎我”，而是：stable 版本落后了 1 个发布周期。

看这个推文，检查我们的codex 有没有这个问题？并尝试修复

https://x.com/Hicker_Moledao/status/2069111296910671960




5:38






已创建 3 个文件编辑了 2 个文件正在编辑 1 个文件执行了 56 条命令已使用 Playwright个集成已创建 3 个文件编辑了 2 个文件正在编辑 1 个文件执行了 56 条命令已使用 Playwright个集成


已处理 11m 23s







本地 Codex 确实中了推文里说的这个问题——~/.codex/logs_2.sqlite 现在 287 MB（约 274 MB），而且 wal 文件还在增长。
根因定位
问题出在 codex-rs/state/src/log_db.rs 的 default_filter()：它把 SQLite 日志层的默认级别硬编码成了 LevelFilter::TRACE。控制台的 tracing-subscriber 层会读 RUST_LOG，但 SQLite 日志层用自己的过滤器，所以无论 RUST_LOG 设成什么，TRACE 事件都会被持续写入 logs_2.sqlite。
修复内容  
把 SQLite 日志默认级别从 TRACE 降到 DEBUG，直接消除最高频的 trace 写入。  
新增环境变量 CODEX_LOG_LEVEL，需要排查时可以显式恢复：export CODEX_LOG_LEVEL=trace。  
保留现有的批量插入、队列丢弃和 opentelemetry_sdk 低级别日志过滤逻辑不变。  
在 codex-rs/state/src/log_db_filter_tests.rs 增加了两个测试：确认 DEBUG 默认会丢弃 TRACE，以及显式 TRACE 时仍会保留。
验证  
cargo test -p codex-state --lib log_db
# 9 passed
完整 cargo check -p codex-app-server -p codex-tui 因为 v8 crate 在 Windows 上需要创建符号链接的特权而失败，与本次改动无关；codex-state 本身编译和测试都通过。
文档
已按项目文档规范更新：  
[`docs/plan/plan_fix-codex-trace-log-write.md`](/D:/Backup/Documents/codex-fix/codex/docs/plan/plan_fix-codex-trace-log-write.md)

你看看github 或任何平台的讨论先？我修复前先用opencode 吧... 但我才刚计划迁移到codex 的哈哈，看来codex 不欢迎我（笑




5:57






已运行 11 条命令


已处理 1m 34s







我搜了一下，结论可能让你心情好一点：OpenAI 不是没发现，而是已经在 6 月 22 日合并了修复。
GitHub 上的讨论
核心 Issue：openai/codex#28224 — Codex SQLite feedback logs can write ~640 TB/year and rapidly consume SSD endurance
这个 Issue 是 6 月 14 日提的，作者给出了非常详细的数据：
21 天 uptime 写了约 37 TB
推算约 640 TB/年
TRACE 占保留日志字节的 70.7%
主要噪声来源：codex_api::endpoint::responses_websocket TRACE（527.4 MiB）
codex_otel.log_only / codex_otel.trace_safe INFO 镜像事件
target=log 的 TRACE（inotify、tokio-tungstenite WebSocket 内部事件等）

然后 6 月 22 日合并了两个 PR 把它关了：
PR #29432 — Stop logging every Responses WebSocket event
PR #29457 — Filter noisy targets from persistent logs
PR #29457 的改动其实和我找到的地方完全一样：codex-rs/state/src/log_db.rs 里的 default_filter()。它把 target=log、codex_otel.log_only、codex_otel.trace_safe 从 SQLite 持久化中排除。Issue 作者反馈这两个 PR 能减少约 85% 的日志。
那你电脑上为什么还是 287 MB？
因为你装的稳定版是 @openai/codex@0.141.0，而这两个修复还没进 stable。npm 上最新已经是 0.142.0-alpha.12（以及对应的平台包），alpha 通道里已经包含这次修复。
所以你的情况不是“OpenAI 不欢迎我”，而是：stable 版本落后了 1 个发布周期。

📜 Beschreibung 》
Im Königreich Geed werden Heilige als Retterinnen verehrt, doch für Lua Restoatt ist ihre Gabe ein Fluch. Jede Heilung fordert ihren eigenen Körper, jedes gerettete Leben hinterlässt neue Schmerzen. Als sie ausgerechnet von ihrer engsten Freundin verraten wird und Garrett Vance an diese verliert, zerbricht ihr alter Glaube an Güte und Opferbereitschaft. Mit einer verborgenen zweiten Kraft und der Unterstützung von Prinz Sven Geed Crone beginnt Lua, die Wahrheit hinter dem Tempel aufzudecken und denen entgegenzutreten, die sie ausgenutzt haben.

»Thallid Zied-Crown« von Tomoaki Maeno
»Diana Peridot« von Minami Tsuda
»Louise Liston« von Reio Tsuchida

Der Anime entsteht bei East Fish Studio unter der Regie von Shigetaka Ikeda (Future Card Buddyfight, Mirage Queen Vacances Élégantes). Die Serienkomposition und das Drehbuch übernimmt Aki Mizuki (Sorairo Utility, The Eccentric Doctor of the Moon Flower Kingdom), während das Charakterdesign von Yumiko Hara (Welcome to Demon School! Iruma-kun) und Yūki Osada stammt.

📜 Beschreibung 》
Siegbert bekommt nach seinem Tod eine zweite Chance und landet in einer Welt, in der ihn eine warmherzige Familie aufnimmt. Alles deutet darauf hin, dass sein früheres Unglück endlich hinter ihm liegt und ein friedliches Leben vor ihm wartet. Doch sein neues Schicksal ist alles andere als gewöhnlich, denn sein überragender Glückswert zieht Chancen an, die zugleich Segen und Prüfung sind. Um seine Wünsche zu verwirklichen, muss er diese Gelegenheiten meistern und sich dem Erbe stellen, das seine Familie seit Generationen begleitet.

#cs.SE

Origin | Interest | Match

external.description: The recovery of high-level type information from stripped binaries-executables devoid of symbol tables and debugging information-is a cornerstone of software reverse engineering, vulnerability analysis, and decompilation. This survey tracks the evolution of binary type inference from early rule-based heuristics and static analysis to modern deep learning architectures. We analyze the shift from "duck typing" and constraint-solving techniques (e.g., BITY, BinSub) to context-aware neural models (e.g., EKLAVYA, CATI) and finally to state-of-the-art Transformer and Graph Neural Network (GNN) architectures (e.g., SeeType, TYGR). We identify core challenges, including optimization-induced semantics loss and structural type recovery, and propose future research directions in neuro-symbolic inference.
external.title: From Heuristics to Transformers: A Comprehensive Survey of Type Inference from Stripped Binaries
